content:
# Coastal Zone Management

**Summary**: The process of creating new land from sea or riverbeds.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2012-al-rowaished|Al-Rowaished, 2012]] studied land-reclamation to quantify its impact on coastal ecosystems.

- [[2016-sudibyo|Sudibyo, 2016]] applied coastal zone management principles to develop oil spill response plans.

- [[2024-ahmad|Ahmad, 2024]] proposed coastal-zone-management strategies to mitigate saltwater intrusion impacts.

## Scientific Context

- [[2012-al-rowaished|Al-Rowaished, 2012]] Land reclamation is a common approach to address land shortages but can damage coastal ecosystems like mangroves.

- [[2016-sudibyo|Sudibyo, 2016]] applied coastal zone management principles to develop oil spill response plans.

- [[2024-ahmad|Ahmad, 2024]] proposed coastal-zone-management strategies to mitigate saltwater intrusion impacts.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2012-al-rowaished|Al-Rowaished, 2012]] Comparison of satellite imagery from different years (1973, 1995, 2007).

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Total reclaimed area in Tarut Bay was 84.42 km² between 1973 and 2007. ([[2012-al-rowaished|Al-Rowaished, 2012, p. 10]])


- Mapping is used for land-use regulation including sensitivity zoning. ([[2016-sudibyo|Sudibyo, 2016, p. 14]])

- Management options include injection wells, seawalls, and water conservation policies. ([[2024-ahmad|Ahmad, 2024, p. 51]])
